name: exercises
description: Craft exercises that students can work on during pairing sessions or as homework, based on the course topics and repository context
---

# Exercises Skill

When this skill is triggered, then we are working together on crafting exercises that students can work on during pairing sessions or as homework, based on the course topics and repository context.

## Guidelines

- The exercises are created in a README.md file in the root of the repository
- The students are not very far in their studies. They only had 1 semester of Python basics. Therefore, the exercises should be crafted in a way that they are easy to understand and do not require too much prior knowledge.
- In a single README, there are multiple exercise blocks
- Each exercise block is structured into the basic exercise itself and a "Level Up" section, which is for early finishers and homework 
- Each exercise block may depend on the basics of previous ones, but not on any "Level Up" exercises
- The students are pair programming on the exercise. If it makes sense, you can make use of this
- For the Level Up, structure them into "Challenges". Give each challenge an appealing and - if reasonable - funny name
- In general, be createive and make the exercises fun to do, while still being educational and relevant to the course topics

## Example Exercises

- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/peterrietzler/ais-dev2il-ais-power-smoothie-delivery-box/refs/heads/main/README.md
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/peterrietzler/ais-dev2il-ais-power-smoothie-maker-ci/refs/heads/main/README.md
